title: Bitter Mediterranean Chicken Liver Falafel
description: A briny, chewy, festive Mediterranean dish featuring chicken liver, dashi-extract, and a mix of spices. Serve with a side of tzatziki or tahini sauce for a refreshing contrast.
instructions: In a food processor, combine chicken liver, dashi-extract, gluten-free flour, fenugreek leaves, garlic, lemon juice, salt, black pepper, paprika, and cayenne pepper. Process until the mixture is well combined and slightly sticky.
Transfer the mixture to a bowl and stir in olive oil until the mixture forms a sticky dough.
Using your hands, shape the dough into small falafel balls, about 1 1/2 inches in diameter.
Heat about 1-2 inches of olive oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat until it reaches 375°F.
Fry the falafel balls in batches until they are golden brown and crispy, about 3-4 minutes per batch.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the falafel from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the falafel hot with your choice of tzatziki or tahini sauce.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley for a pop of color and freshness.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
